stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
Key associated technologies include transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under text-generation pipelines, indicating its primary function as a generative AI model.
The associated tags (transformers, safetensors, llama) confirm its relevance within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: WhatsApp ordered to host rival AI assistants for free
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: European Commission ordered Meta to allow rival AI assistants on WhatsApp for free.
The measure is an interim step during an antitrust investigation into Meta's market practices.
The regulator cited the need to prevent 'serious and irreparable damage to competition' in the AI assistant market.

stories.title: Anthropic says these topics are too dangerous to let its Fable 5 model talk about
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic's Fable 5 model implements safety guardrails against dangerous topics.
The model explicitly refuses queries related to cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry.
This highlights the ongoing industry focus on AI safety and restricting harmful capabilities.

stories.title: Google announces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate for instant voice-to-voice translation
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google introduced Gemini 3.5 Live Translate for real-time voice-to-voice translation.
The new feature preserves nuanced vocal characteristics like tone, pacing, and pitch.
Security is addressed through the integration of SynthID watermarking technology.

stories.title: One day after discovery, Meta pulls facial recognition code from its smart glasses
stories.author: Dhruv Mehrotra and Dell Cameron, wired.com
bullet_points: Meta has removed facial recognition code from its smart glasses shortly after the feature was discovered.
The company has not provided a reason for the removal or indicated if the feature will be reinstated.
This action highlights ongoing privacy concerns and regulatory scrutiny surrounding facial recognition technology in consumer hardware.

stories.title: ABC-Bench: An Agentic Bio-Capabilities Benchmark for Biosecurity
stories.author: Seth Donoughe
bullet_points: LLMs are developing significant capabilities in biological research, including data interpretation and in silico biology tasks.
The rise of these AI capabilities presents both opportunities for scientific discovery and new biosecurity risks.
The paper introduces ABC-Bench, a benchmark designed to evaluate the agentic bio-capabilities of these models.

stories.title: German ruling declares Google liable for false answers in AI Overviews
stories.author: ahlCVA
bullet_points: A German ruling holds Google legally responsible for inaccuracies presented in its AI Overviews.
This signals increasing regulatory scrutiny over the factual reliability of generative AI outputs.
The case represents a significant legal challenge to the deployment and liability of large language models.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on model deployment and comparison.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on large language model capabilities.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors, pointing to standard modern NLP tooling.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in preview, signaling a push for local, efficient AI deployment.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local operation.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running AI entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models such as Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Waymo built a virtual driver to study how humans react to surprises on the road
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Waymo is utilizing virtual simulation environments to enhance autonomous vehicle capabilities.
The company created a virtual representation of a hyperattentive human driver for testing.
This research aims to help AVs better anticipate and react to unpredictable real-world events and surprises.

stories.title: GM thinks EVs can help offset AI’s energy suck with vehicle-to-grid tech
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: GM is leveraging Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) technology to address increasing energy demands from AI data centers.
The automaker announced the activation of new V2G capabilities for its existing EV and home energy customers.
The announcement signals a growing industry focus on grid resiliency and integrating distributed energy resources (like EVs) into the power infrastructure.

stories.title: Microsoft AI head calls out Anthropic for acting like Claude is conscious
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman criticized Anthropic for speculating about Claude's consciousness.
Suleyman warned that such speculation within the model's 'constitution' could be 'really, really dangerous'.
The discussion highlights the growing industry debate over AI sentience and the implications of model alignment/instructions.

stories.title: Commonwealth Fusion makes the physics case for its 400 MW reactor
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Commonwealth Fusion has provided updated physics modeling for its 400 MW reactor design.
The progress is substantiated by five new peer-reviewed scientific papers.
This represents a significant step in the development of advanced fusion energy technology.

stories.title: NASA assigns crew for Artemis III, sets aggressive timeline for flying it
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA has assigned the crew for the Artemis III mission.
The mission is set with an aggressive timeline for execution.
Artemis III is framed as a major demonstration of advanced technological capability.

stories.title: Data Journalist Agent: Transforming Data into Verifiable Multimodal Stories
stories.author: James Zou
bullet_points: Introduces a 'Data Journalist Agent' aiming to automate the complex, multi-step process of turning raw data into trustworthy, multimodal news stories.
Addresses the current limitations of existing agents, which excel at isolated tasks (e.g., data analysis or visual design) but lack end-to-end journalistic capability.
Represents a significant step toward autonomous AI systems capable of complex, high-level cognitive tasks in content creation and information synthesis.

stories.title: PhantomBench: Benchmarking the Non-existential Threat of Language Models
stories.author: Hila Gonen
bullet_points: Introduces PhantomBench, a new large-scale benchmark specifically designed to test language model hallucinations.
Addresses the critical risk of LMs generating factually ungrounded responses, especially in high-stakes applications.
Focuses on the challenge of determining how reliably LMs can recognize the boundaries of their own knowledge.

stories.title: The Shibboleth Effect: Auditing the Cross-Lingual Distributional Skew of Large Language Models
stories.author: Hakan Mehmetcik
bullet_points: Investigates the 'Shibboleth Effect,' which is cross-lingual distributional skew in frontier LLMs.
Uses a novel, complex synthetic geopolitical wargame ('Cerulean Sea Crisis') to test models under adversarial conditions.
Compares six leading frontier models (including GPT-4o, Llama-4, Gemini-3.1-Pro, etc.) for bias and performance in cross-lingual scenarios.

stories.title: Mercedes‑Benz starts large‑scale production of electric axial flux motor
stories.author: raffael_de
bullet_points: Mercedes-Benz is initiating mass production of a new electric axial flux motor.
This signals a significant advancement in automotive electrification technology.
The adoption of axial flux motors represents a key shift in EV powertrain design.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code with open-source models via Ollama.
It bridges the gap between local, open-source LLM deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: Microsoft is disabling Office 2019 for Mac next month
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is discontinuing support for Office 2019 applications on macOS next month.
The discontinuation is due to Microsoft ceasing the renewal of a necessary license validation certificate.
Users must upgrade to Office 2024 or a Microsoft 365 subscription to maintain functionality.

stories.title: The App Store is going to add subscription bundles soon
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Apple is expanding App Store bundles to allow for combined subscription offers.
These new bundles can feature subscriptions from multiple, different companies.
The feature is expected to roll out later this year, similar to existing streaming service bundles.

stories.title: Starlink charges $10 monthly hardware fee in move away from one-time purchases
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Starlink is implementing a new business model by charging a $10 monthly hardware fee.
Service subscription prices for Starlink have also been increased by $5, now totaling $10.
This represents a strategic shift for SpaceX's primary revenue stream, Starlink.

stories.title: Locked in heated rivalry with researcher, Microsoft fixes 0-day they disclosed
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Microsoft has reportedly patched a zero-day vulnerability that was disclosed by a researcher.
The news highlights a competitive dynamic between the researcher and Microsoft regarding vulnerability disclosure.
Another zero-day vulnerability, disclosed by 'Nightmare Eclipse,' also appears to have been patched.

stories.title: A Unifying Lens on Supervised Fine-Tuning Through Target Distribution Design
stories.author: Cho-Jui Hsieh
bullet_points: The paper reinterprets Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) not just as likelihood maximization, but as a problem of target distribution design.
It argues that strictly fitting to observed tokens can be suboptimal because demonstrations may be noisy or non-unique.
The research analyzes the token-level target distribution that the loss function implicitly drives during SFT.

stories.title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents
stories.author: Mengdi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces EEVEE, a novel framework for test-time prompt learning in LLM agents.
Addresses a key limitation in current methods: the inability to handle heterogeneous, multi-dataset real-world task streams.
Employs a router mechanism to mitigate cross-dataset interference, enhancing practical applicability.

stories.title: Piper: A Programmable Distributed Training System
stories.author: Stephanie Wang
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Piper,' a system designed to manage complex, composed parallelism strategies (data, pipeline, expert) used in large-scale model training.
Current foundation model training often requires manual, expert-driven design of high-level parallelism strategies, leading to inflexibility.
Piper aims to automate or simplify the process of implementing these complex, multi-strategy execution plans, addressing current framework limitations.

stories.title: Flaws in the LLM Automation Narrative
stories.author: Marc Scott
bullet_points: The narrative claiming LLMs match human experts in knowledge tasks is being questioned.
Current benchmarks often measure performance on data already present in the LLM's training set.
A key limitation of existing evaluations is the lack of assessment regarding LLM performance reliability or magnitude.

stories.title: ReasonAlloc: Hierarchical Decoding-Time KV Cache Budget Allocation for Reasoning Models
stories.author: Shuang Qiu
bullet_points: Addresses the inference bottleneck caused by rapid Key-Value (KV) cache growth during long Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning in LLMs.
Proposes 'ReasonAlloc,' a novel method for hierarchical, decoding-time KV cache budget allocation.
Overcomes limitations of existing methods by moving beyond uniform budget distribution and addressing stepwise context demands.

stories.title: When to Align, When to Predict: A Phase Diagram for Multimodal Learning
stories.author: Randall Balestriero
bullet_points: The paper addresses a critical gap in multimodal representation learning by analyzing when Cross-modal Alignment (CA) versus Cross-modal Prediction (CP) is more effective.
It proposes a 'Phase Diagram' to systematically guide practitioners on selecting the appropriate training paradigm for multimodal tasks.
The research is highly relevant to scientific domains (e.g., biomedicine, astrophysics) where heterogeneous data and complex measurements are common.

stories.title: A Unifying Lens on Supervised Fine-Tuning Through Target Distribution Design
stories.author: Cho-Jui Hsieh
bullet_points: The paper reinterprets Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) from a simple likelihood maximization to a 'target distribution design' problem.
It argues that strictly fitting to observed tokens in SFT can be suboptimal due to noise, non-uniqueness, or misalignment.
The work analyzes the token-level target distribution that the loss function implicitly drives the model toward.

stories.title: EEVEE: Towards Test-time Prompt Learning in the Real World for Self-Improving Agents
stories.author: Mengdi Wang
bullet_points: Introduces EEVEE, a novel multi-dataset test-time prompt learning framework for LLM agents.
Addresses a key limitation in current methods: the inability to handle heterogeneous, real-world task streams from multiple datasets.
Utilizes a router mechanism within EEVEE to mitigate cross-dataset interference during learning.

stories.title: Predicting Future Behaviors in Reasoning Models Enables Better Steering
stories.author: Wojciech Samek
bullet_points: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) often exhibit unpredictable behavior during deployment.
Traditional 'test-time steering' methods, which intervene on hidden representations, can degrade output quality.
The research proposes training activation probes to predict future behavioral outcomes, suggesting a better target for model steering than current internal features.

stories.title: COGENT: Continuous Graph Emulators with Neural Ordinary Differential Equations for Long-Term Physical Forecasting
stories.author: Maryam Rahnemoonfar
bullet_points: Introduces COGENT, a new continuous graph emulator designed for long-term physical forecasting.
It utilizes Neural Ordinary Differential Equations (NODEs) to model temporal evolution on irregular geospatial meshes.
The system employs a graph-based history encoder to capture local spatial interactions and system state history.

stories.title: Itô maps for any-step SDEs
stories.author: Jakiw Pidstrigach
bullet_points: Introduces the 'Itô map,' a novel stochastic flow map capable of predicting future states from an intermediate state and Brownian path in one pass.
Addresses a limitation in current generative models that rely on ODEs, by providing a framework for handling stochastic dynamics (SDEs).
The formulation yields new estimators relevant for inference-time control applications in stochastic systems.

stories.title: OncoTraj: a public benchmark for longitudinal resistance prediction in EGFR-mutant non-small-cell lung cancer on osimertinib
stories.author: Aarchi Singh Thakur
bullet_points: Introduces OncoTraj, a new public benchmark dataset for studying cancer resistance.
Focuses specifically on predicting longitudinal resistance in EGFR-mutant NSCLC treated with osimertinib.
Harmonizes data from three real-world clinical-genomic sources, providing a valuable resource for ML model training.

stories.title: TRACE: A Unified Rollout Budget Allocation Framework for Efficient Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Xiangyang Ji
bullet_points: The paper introduces TRACE, a framework designed to optimize budget allocation for agentic reinforcement learning.
It addresses a key limitation in Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR): low-variance feedback during rollout-intensive policy optimization.
The core problem tackled is that simple or complex prompts, or outcome-only rewards, lead to insufficient reward contrast in multi-turn rollouts.

stories.title: Modeling Complex Behaviors: Multi-Personality Composition and Dynamic Switching in Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Zhou Su
bullet_points: The research addresses the need to control complex behaviors in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) for social interaction.
It introduces explicit personality conditioning and a systematic evaluation framework for single, multi, and switching personalities.
Experiments indicate that while personality induction improves image captioning, it can negatively impact performance.

stories.title: Rich Sutton on AI creativity and discovery
stories.author: yimby
bullet_points: The content features Rich Sutton, a prominent figure in AI research.
The discussion centers on the concepts of AI creativity and discovery.
The source is a YouTube video linked from Hacker News, suggesting expert commentary rather than a direct breakthrough announcement.

stories.title: Ultrafast machine learning on FPGAs via Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
stories.author: ag2718
bullet_points: The research focuses on achieving ultrafast machine learning computations.
The implementation leverages Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) for hardware acceleration.
The core innovation involves the use of Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) in this context.

stories.title: CVE-2026-45447: Heap Use-After-Free in the OpenSSL PKCS7_verify() Function
stories.author: openssl-library.org via hoistbypetard
bullet_points: The news reports a specific vulnerability, CVE-2026-45447, affecting OpenSSL.
The vulnerability is classified as a Heap Use-After-Free flaw within the PKCS7_verify() function.
This is a technical security advisory, indicating potential risks for systems utilizing OpenSSL.

stories.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now Generally Available on Google Cloud! 🚀
stories.author: pugsandprincesses
bullet_points: Claude Fable 5, Anthropic's latest model, is now generally available.
The model is specifically available for deployment on Google Cloud.
This release is relevant for developers utilizing Anthropic's AI services on GCP.

stories.title: RAG-Based Testing Series — Part 1: What Is RAG & Why Your Old Testing Playbook Won't Work Here
stories.author: sshhfaiz
bullet_points: The article introduces Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems.
It argues that traditional testing methodologies are insufficient for RAG applications.
The content promises a comprehensive series leading to an automated RAG testing framework.
